data.description:
## Context

Pattern 3 (identical-root-cause regressions, ~10% of recent fixes) is the dominant repeat-bug class. The /dso:fix-bug skill mandates intent-search for the same ticket's history but does not require a tree-wide scan for the same antipattern after the fix. The prior-art protocol (prior-art-search.md:79-88) explicitly excludes single-file fixes — exactly the case where Pattern 3 manifests. Many Pattern 1 bugs were originally reproducible only in CI because the local repro was contaminated by inherited environment.

This epic adds two complementary fix-bug discipline additions:
1. Tree-wide antipattern scan after every fix, with the antipattern promoted to the known-antipatterns registry delivered by New Epic A
2. Hermetic-repro requirement for the RED test in fix-bug Step 5

See docs/observed-failure-patterns-4-26.md Patterns 1, 3 and docs/process-gaps-4-26.md G6, G9.

## Cited evidence

Pattern 3 recurrence chain (the empirical proof):
- 09d8-11f0 (commit e913a71d5a) — pre-commit-format-fix.sh CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT under set -u
- fe45-0b58 (commit dcbe98f3ef) — skip-review-check.sh, same root cause, two months later. Ticket description explicitly cites 09d8-11f0.
- 82ad-7bb3 (commit f5a055d1fa) — bash-runner.sh, same root cause again
- 97a7-4504 (commit 495c8c4b4f) — annotation follow-up on bash-runner.sh, same root cause
- Each ticket was treated as a single-file fix and excluded from prior-art search by the prior-art-search.md:79-88 "Routine exclusions" rule

SIGPIPE recurrence:
- e241-41b2 — bulk replacement across 26 files in commits b9358f95eb + 0113ab66d9
- 999e-cf69 — a 27th instance discovered later in ticket-push-rebase-conflict. Single-file scope, missed by the 26-file sweep because no tree-wide ratchet existed.

declare -A recurrence:
- 4191-9096 and 0a47-9631 — two separate scripts, same fix, different times

Hermeticity (G9) — fix-bug missing hermetic repro:
- .github/workflows/ci.yml has no env: block clearing CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT; validate.sh:87-88 fallback for unset variable is therefore never exercised in CI
- Pattern 1 bugs (fe45-0b58, 09d8-11f0, 82ad-7bb3) were originally caught by CI in a partially hermetic environment, then "fixed" against a contaminated local environment, then re-broken by subsequent changes. A hermetic-repro requirement at fix-bug time would have surfaced the contamination immediately.

Prior-art exclusion gap (G6):
- prior-art-search.md:79-88 — quoted verbatim: "Routine exclusions: single-file logic fixes, formatting/lint, test reversions, doc-only edits, config value updates."
- plugins/dso/skills/fix-bug/SKILL.md and plugins/dso/skills/debug-everything/SKILL.md — neither mandates a tree-wide scan after a fix lands

## Mitigations included

- G6-M1 — /dso:fix-bug mandatory post-fix step (after fix verification, before commit): extract the antipattern as a regex or sg AST query (e.g., \$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT[^:-] for the unguarded-env-var pattern). Run query across the entire codebase. If matches exist outside the fixed file: open follow-up bug tickets for each match cluster, OR include matches in current fix scope, OR add # antipattern-ok: <reason> annotation to each. Commit message must include the antipattern query and match count.
- G9-M3 — /dso:fix-bug Step 5 (RED test) requires the failing test be reproducible in env -i bash -c 'unset CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT; <test command>'. If it does not reproduce hermetically, the test is environmental noise and the bug needs reframing.

## Proposed success criteria

1. /dso:fix-bug SKILL.md adds a post-fix Step 7.5 (after fix verification, before commit) documenting the antipattern scan procedure with exact sg and grep invocations
2. Commit messages from /dso:fix-bug include an Antipattern-Scan: <query> matches=<n> trailer; /dso:commit workflow reads the trailer and refuses to commit a fix-bug change without it (or with matches > 0 and no follow-up plan)
3. /dso:fix-bug Step 5 enforces hermetic-repro check; non-hermetic RED tests block progression to Step 6
4. The known-antipatterns registry (delivered by New Epic A) is the receiving home for queries surfaced by Step 7.5; this epic adds a one-line "promote to registry" sub-step in fix-bug
5. Coverage: re-running the procedure against the cited Pattern 3 chain (09d8-11f0 → fe45-0b58 → 82ad-7bb3 → 97a7-4504) collapses the chain into a single fix; documented as a regression scenario

## Coordination notes

- Hard dependency on New Epic A for the antipattern registry (G6-M2). Sequence: A ships the registry; E populates it.
- Soft pairing with New Epic B's rubric items: reviewers under the new B rubric will flag antipatterns the fix-bug Step 7.5 missed
- Coexists with c183-ed2a's adversarial review of inference-sourced decisions — different layer (fix-time vs. plan-time challenge)

## References

- docs/observed-failure-patterns-4-26.md Patterns 1, 3
- docs/process-gaps-4-26.md G6, G9
- docs/proposed-mitigations-4-26.md G6-M1, G9-M3
- docs/new-epics-4-26.md § New Epic E
data.title: fix-bug recurrence prevention: tree-wide antipattern scan and hermetic repro
